title: service-accounts — platform-ops analytics
description: Analytics events on the machine-credential plane. These are Academorix-internal platform-ops events — they land in the SAME internal Segment source as platform-user events (SEGMENT_INTERNAL_WRITE_KEY), never in the tenant-facing product source. Consent tier is `essential` — platform-ops analytics are internal operational metrics governed by SOC 2 CC7.2, not by the tenant-plane consent framework. No SA secret material ever appears on any event.

events.name: Service Account Created
events.description: New SA provisioned. `tenant_id` is nullable (null = platform-plane SA). `expires_at` drives the rotation-due dashboards. Feeds the machine-credential inventory dashboard.

events.name: Service Account Secret Rotated
events.description: Secret rotated. `trigger` = manual|scheduled|incident|expired_grace. `days_since_last_rotation` feeds the rotation-hygiene dashboard — target median < ttl_days, tail warning > 1.5x ttl.

events.name: Service Account Disabled
events.description: SA soft-disabled. `trigger` = manual|expired|dormant|suspicious_activity|incident. Feeds the credential-hygiene dashboard — dormant + expired triggers should tail down as ops adopts rotation discipline.

events.name: Service Account Enabled
events.description: SA re-enabled after having been disabled. `disabled_duration_days` correlates with incident-recovery time; long durations may indicate the SA should have been deleted rather than disabled.

events.name: Service Account Deleted
events.description: SA soft-deleted (start of the retention hold). `lifetime_days` counts from `created_at`. `usage_last_day_count` reads the last 24h `last_used_at` velocity so ops can catch accidentally deleting an actively-used SA.

events.name: Service Account JWT Issued
events.description: Token-exchange endpoint issued a JWT. Feeds the JWT-issuance-rate dashboard — anomalies + spikes correlate with either a fleet scale-out (benign) or a compromised-credential replay (malignant). Sampled: 1 event per 100 successful issuances by default to keep event volume manageable.

events.name: Service Account Rotation Due Notified
events.description: NotifyRotationDueJob dispatched the warning notification. Feeds the rotation-followup dashboard — SAs with a Rotation Due Notified event but no Secret Rotated event within `days_until_expiry` become the rotation-noncompliance queue.

events.name: Service Account Expired
events.description: expires_at + grace period elapsed with no rotation — SA soft-disabled by PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob. `last_used_at_days_ago` distinguishes 'genuinely forgotten' (large value) from 'in-use but ops missed the rotation' (small value).

events.name: Service Account Dormant Detected
events.description: DetectDormantServiceAccountJob flagged an unused SA. Feeds the credential-inventory-cleanup dashboard; the security team triages via the Sunday-morning report.

events.name: Service Account Usage Detected After Dormancy
events.description: Fired when a SA that has been silent >30 days makes a request. Distinct from the periodic Dormant Detected — this signals a WAKE UP, which is a compromise-adjacent signal. Feeds the security triage queue.

events.name: Service Account Suspicious Activity Detected
events.description: Fingerprinter caught a rate-spike + novel-tuple combination. `signal_type` = rate_spike_novel_ip | rate_spike_novel_ua | high_failure_rate | cross_kid_signature_reuse. Feeds the security incident-response pipeline.

notes: None of these events carry `secret_hash`, `signer_kid` (the kid value itself IS included for auditability but is not a secret), plaintext secrets, or JWT payloads.
`actor_platform_user_id` is always a PlatformUser id (never a User id, never a ServiceAccount id). Every mutating event has a human operator behind it.
`request_ip_prefix_24` truncates the IP to the /24 network. Sufficient for anomaly correlation, insufficient for surveillance.
Segment write key resolves via env `SEGMENT_INTERNAL_WRITE_KEY` (distinct from `SEGMENT_TENANT_WRITE_KEY`). Same source as platform-user events.
